commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: Android release: bring the chat experience much closer to the WebUI
target.body:
## Summary

The Android chat in the base OpenClaw build was usable, but it still felt like a much simpler version of the WebUI. This PR closes a big part of that gap.

The main goal here was straightforward: make chat on Android feel like a real OpenClaw client, not a reduced mobile companion. That meant bringing over the pieces that make the WebUI chat actually convenient to use day to day — better navigation between agents and sessions, more visibility into what the model is doing, better control over what is shown in the timeline, and a much more readable session list.

В базовой Android-сборке OpenClaw чат был рабочим, но по ощущениям всё ещё заметно уступал WebUI. Он был слишком упрощён: меньше контроля, меньше контекста, слабее работа с session и agent-ами, хуже читаемость списка чатов.

В этой сборке мы целенаправленно подтянули Android именно в сторону полноценного WebUI-like chat experience. Цель была простая: чтобы с телефона OpenClaw был не «урезанным компаньоном», а действительно удобным chat-клиентом.

## What users get

With this build, Android chat becomes much more practical to use:

- you can switch between workspaces / agents from the chat UI
- you can work with sessions through a proper dropdown instead of a much thinner flow
- you can create a new chat and delete the current one from the app
- you can choose how much extra chat detail you want to see:
  - assistant thinking / working output
  - tool results
- you get more useful runtime context in the chat itself:
  - model fallback notices
  - context usage notices
  - compaction status
- Telegram-created chats, threads, and topics are named much more cleanly
- old sessions are no longer hidden behind the previous hard 24-hour cutoff
- technical or noisy metadata labels no longer dominate the session list

Теперь Android-чатом стало гораздо удобнее пользоваться:

- можно переключаться между workspace / agent-ами прямо из интерфейса чата
- можно нормально работать с session через выпадающий список
- можно создавать новый чат и удалять текущий
- можно выбирать, сколько дополнительной информации показывать в чате:
  - размышления / working output модели
  - результаты tools
- прямо в интерфейсе стало больше полезного runtime-контекста:
  - предупреждения о model fallback
  - заметки о заполнении context window
  - статус compaction
- Telegram-чаты, темы и треды стали называться гораздо чище и понятнее
- старые session больше не скрываются из-за прежнего жёсткого 24-часового cutoff
- технические и шумные metadata-label’ы больше не забивают список session

## In practice

The Android chat now feels much closer to the WebUI in the places that actually matter.

It is easier to move between agents. It is easier to understand which session you are in. It is easier to read Telegram-driven chats without weird labels. And it is easier to see what the model or tools are doing without turning the app into a wall of technical noise.

A lot of supporting stabilization work also went into this PR — especially around session actions and delete flows — but that work is here mainly to support the bigger goal above: making Android chat feel fuller, clearer, and more useful.

На практике Android теперь гораздо ближе к WebUI именно там, где это действительно важно.

Стало проще переключаться между agent-ами. Проще понимать, в какой session ты находишься. Проще читать Telegram-историю без странных названий. Проще видеть, что делает модель и что возвращают tools, не превращая чат в техническую свалку.

В PR также вошла поддерживающая стабилизация — особенно вокруг session actions и delete flow, — но она здесь не как главная история, а как опорный слой под основную цель: сделать Android-чат более полным, понятным и удобным.

## Validation

Focused Android regression tests passed:
- `ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest`
- `SessionFiltersTest`
- `ChatSheetContentTest`
- `GatewayBootstrapAuthTest`
- `GatewaySessionInvokeTest`
- `GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`
- `ConnectionManagerTest`

Final validated APK:
- `2026.4.22-beta.10`
- SHA-256: `6d79031a85d68e75da47c3832ce63541fc5a6115b801db4aaa60ad061b6b9af2`

Сфокусированные Android regression tests пройдены:
- `ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest`
- `SessionFiltersTest`
- `ChatSheetContentTest`
- `GatewayBootstrapAuthTest`
- `GatewaySessionInvokeTest`
- `GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`
- `ConnectionManagerTest`

Финально проверенный APK:
- `2026.4.22-beta.10`
- SHA-256: `6d79031a85d68e75da47c3832ce63541fc5a6115b801db4aaa60ad061b6b9af2`

## Out of scope
- signed Android release packaging is still gated by local signing secrets

Не входит в этот PR:
- signed Android release packaging по-прежнему зависит от локальных signing secrets

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Android chat model selection no longer races the next send. When the user selects a model and immediately sends a message, the Android controller waits for the in-flight `sessions.patch` model update before `chat.send`; if the patch fails, the send is rejected instead of silently using the previous model. This also keeps the composer/model picker disabled while the session action is in flight.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Android test environment on Linux 5.15.0-176-generic x64, branch `pr-70993-review-fix`, merged with `origin/main` at `2d65908f7fa0c0b543229f71b509408185c91926`, Android SDK at `/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk`, Gradle/Android unit tests executed from `apps/android` via the repository `./gradlew` wrapper.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.chat.ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`; then `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; then `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest`; plus `PATH="$PWD/.tmp-corepack-bin:$PATH" ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --passWithNoTests=false` for the resolved TypeScript conflict.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the after-fix local run:
  ```text
  $ ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.chat.ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest
  BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 35s

  $ ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck
  BUILD SUCCESSFUL

  $ ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest
  BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 43s

  $ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
  no_conflict_markers=0

  $ git diff --check origin/main -- CHANGELOG.md apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/chat/ChatController.kt apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/ui/chat/ChatComposer.kt apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/ui/chat/ChatSheetContent.kt apps/android/app/src/test/java/ai/openclaw/app/chat/ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest.kt apps/android/app/src/test/java/ai/openclaw/app/gateway/GatewaySessionInvokeTest.kt src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
  scoped_diff_check=0

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --passWithNoTests=false
  ✓ auto-reply-reply src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts (83 tests) 3347ms
  Test Files 1 passed (1)
  Tests 83 passed (83)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The focused Android controller tests exercised the select-model-then-immediately-send path and passed: `sendWaitsForSelectedModelPatchBeforeChatSend` verifies `chat.send` waits until `sessions.patch` completes, and `sendIsRejectedWhenSelectedModelPatchFails` verifies no `chat.send` is issued after a failed model patch. The full Android unit gate and ktlint also passed after merging current `origin/main`; the resolved `session.test.ts` conflict passed its 83-test auto-reply shard.
- What was not tested: No physical Android device/manual tap-through recording was captured; this proof is from local after-fix terminal output and focused Android controller/unit coverage.
target.author: svetznaniy33
